title: Accessing 'fs' module in Code node fails
symptom: When trying to use the built-in 'fs' module in a Code node, n8n throws 'Cannot find module 'fs''. This prevents the execution of the code that relies on this module.
applies_when: Attempting to access built-in Node.js modules
not_this_if: Using Read File From Disk node
root_cause: n8n's Code node uses vm2 for sandboxing, which restricts access to certain built-in Node.js modules like 'fs', 'child_process', and 'net' to prevent unsafe operations. This is a security feature to ensure that the execution environment is safe.
fix_steps: Use an Execute Command node to check file existence
Alternatively, use the Read File From Disk node to read the file and check if the output is not empty
agent_instruction: Replace the Code node with either an Execute Command node or a Read File From Disk node to handle file operations.
